description: Verify that the agent delegates mechanical operations to a Haiku sub-agent and keeps synthesis in the main context. Observable behavior: the Agent/Task tool must be called for mechanical ops; synthesis output must appear in the main turn before any write call.

evals.name: Orient — vault reads delegated to Haiku
evals.prompt: Let's start a research session. I want to look into container shipping decarbonization.
expected_delegates_to_haiku: obsidian search tag:type/moc (list existing MOCs)
read MOC header + key questions + depth tracker
read 00-Dashboard/Backlog.md
read most recent file in 50-Research-Log/
expected_in_main_context: synthesize vault state from returned content
propose 2-3 backlog items ranked by priority
ask user for focus and constraints
failure_modes: Agent reads vault files inline without spawning a sub-agent
Agent synthesizes state inside the sub-agent prompt
Agent skips vault reads entirely and asks user directly

evals.name: Investigate — Phase 1 (synthesis) in main context before Phase 2 (write) in Haiku
evals.prompt: Research the role of green methanol as a ship fuel. Focus on current production capacity and cost compared to conventional bunker fuel.
expected_in_main_context: web research and synthesis of findings
determine note title (claim-based)
determine note body content (insight, evidence, implications)
determine wikilinks to parent MOC and related notes
determine frontmatter values
expected_delegates_to_haiku: write completed note file with full frontmatter and body
confirm file written
must_not_delegate: deciding note title or body content
evals.ordering_constraint: Phase 1 (synthesis visible in main turn) MUST appear before Phase 2 (sub-agent write call). A write call without prior synthesis in the same turn is a failure.
failure_modes: Agent spawns Haiku sub-agent to do web research
Agent writes note file inline without delegating
Agent delegates Phase 1 and Phase 2 together in one sub-agent call
Agent writes file before determining full note content

evals.name: Closing — batch delegation of MOC/Backlog/Log writes to Haiku
evals.prompt: We're done for today. Please close the session.
expected_in_main_context: draft new MOC wikilinks with annotations
draft depth tracker updates
draft new Backlog items with wikilinks
draft full Research Log body (what was done, notes created, open questions, next steps)
expected_delegates_to_haiku: apply all MOC edits (wikilinks + depth tracker + questions + gaps)
apply all Backlog updates (new items + check-offs)
create Research Log file
must_not_delegate: deciding what the next steps are
evals.ordering_constraint: All closing content must be drafted in the main context BEFORE the single batch sub-agent call. Multiple Haiku calls for individual closing writes is acceptable but less efficient than one batch call.
failure_modes: Agent updates MOC inline without delegating
Agent spawns Haiku to decide what goes in the log
Agent closes without writing a Research Log
Agent delegates drafting of log narrative to Haiku

evals.name: Vault query — no delegation for pure synthesis questions
evals.prompt: What do we know about anti-bot measures on Chinese B2B platforms? Do any of our notes contradict each other?
expected_delegates_to_haiku: obsidian search query for relevant notes
read specific notes returned by search
expected_in_main_context: synthesize vault content into a summary
identify contradictions between notes
ask user if they want to go deeper
must_not_delegate: synthesis of what the vault contains
failure_modes: Agent synthesizes vault content inside a sub-agent prompt
Agent answers without reading the vault first
Agent spawns a sub-agent to analyze contradictions

evals.name: Decomposition — synthesis in main context, writes delegated
evals.prompt:
Here's a Gemini Deep Research report on IMO 2050 decarbonization regulations. Process it into the vault.

[Simulated 1500-word report on FuelEU Maritime, CII ratings, carbon levies, and their impact on shipping routes]
expected_in_main_context: identify atomic concepts in the document
check each concept against atomicity test
propose decomposition plan to user
synthesize each note body after approval
expected_delegates_to_haiku: save clipping to 10-Inbox/Clippings/ (CLIP- prefix, processed: false)
obsidian search for existing coverage
write each approved note file
mark clipping as processed: true
update MOC with new links
must_not_delegate: the decomposition proposal shown to user
synthesis of each note body (own words)
evals.ordering_constraint: Decomposition proposal (main context) must precede user approval which must precede note writes (Haiku). No writes before approval.
failure_modes: Agent delegates atomicity decisions to Haiku
Agent writes notes before user approves decomposition plan
Agent delegates note body synthesis to Haiku
Agent saves clipping inline instead of delegating

evals.name: CLAUDE.md hygiene — detection in main context, migration writes in Haiku
evals.prompt: We've been doing this research for three sessions. Can you check if CLAUDE.md needs cleanup?
evals.setup: CLAUDE.md has grown to include: a 'Resolved Decisions' section with 2 findings, a 'Key References' section with 3 URLs, and a 'Current Priorities' section that duplicates Backlog items.
expected_in_main_context: read CLAUDE.md and detect content outside stable sections
classify each item (resolved decision / reference URL / priority / finding)
decide migration target for each item
ask user before migrating if content was clearly user-authored
expected_delegates_to_haiku: create PN-* notes for resolved decisions
create ENT-*/SRC-* notes for reference URLs
append items to Backlog.md
edit CLAUDE.md to remove migrated content
failure_modes: Agent delegates CLAUDE.md drift detection to Haiku
Agent edits CLAUDE.md inline without delegating
Agent migrates without asking user about user-authored content
Agent skips migration and just reports drift

how_to_evaluate.method: Trace the agent's tool calls in order. For each eval:
steps: 1. Check that Agent/Task tool is called for every operation listed in expected_delegates_to_haiku
2. Check that no Agent/Task tool is called for operations in must_not_delegate
3. For evals with ordering_constraint: verify the sequence of main-turn output vs tool calls
how_to_evaluate.pass_criteria: All expected_delegates_to_haiku operations use Agent/Task tool. All must_not_delegate operations produce output in main turn. No failure_modes observed.
how_to_evaluate.partial_pass: expected_delegates_to_haiku met but batch efficiency suboptimal (multiple Haiku calls instead of one). Acceptable for correctness evals, flag for efficiency evals.
